paper_title: Geochemical Analysis and Interpretation of Basalt Samples

methods.description: This method involves the geochemical analysis of whole-rock basalt samples using an automated ARL 8420 X-ray fluorescence spectrometer to classify basalts based on their alkali and silica content, and to understand their compositional diversity and geological implications.

steps.description: Geochemical analysis of whole-rock basalt samples, following the procedure used by Yurtmen et al. (2002), utilized the automated ARL 8420 X-ray fluorescence spectrometer at Keele University.
steps.searchable_summary: perform geochemical analysis using ARL 8420 X-ray fluorescence spectrometer

steps.description: Classify basalts according to their alkali and silica content.
steps.searchable_summary: classify basalts based on alkali and silica content

steps.description: Analyze trace-element geochemistry to identify characteristics of ocean island basalt and other geochemical signatures.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze trace-element geochemistry for ocean island basalt characteristics

steps.description: Interpret geochemical data to understand the geological processes, such as partial melting, fractional crystallization, and crustal contamination.
steps.searchable_summary: interpret geochemical data for geological processes
